content: Autonomous curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents can be re-engineered to treat working memory capacity limits not as a bug to be fixed, but as a feature that directs intrinsic curiosity toward compressing and consolidating new information that fills unaddressed memory gaps. This creates a self-reinforcing active learning loop where capacity constraints drive targeted exploration that improves long-term reasoning performance, rather than limiting it.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
